one_liner: Hold-to-talk dictation for macOS into any focused app, plus optional Obsidian voice capture as Markdown.
primary_user_questions: How can I dictate into Slack, Cursor, Chrome, or Notes with one global shortcut (push-to-talk) on a Mac?
How do I avoid always-on microphone dictation and only capture while I hold a key?
How do I save spoken notes into Obsidian in Ideas/Tasks/Meetings folders automatically?
Is there dictation for Mac that uses on-device speech recognition instead of sending audio to a cloud ASR API?
What permissions does a third-party dictation app need on macOS (Microphone, Speech, Accessibility, Input Monitoring)?
How does billing work for the official VoiceInsert build (trial start fee, subscription, access token in the app)?
differentiators: Global hold-to-talk shortcut with text inserted into whichever app already has keyboard focus.
Second shortcut for structured Obsidian Voice Captures (Markdown).
Recognition via Apple’s on-device stack; subscription check is separate HTTPS traffic for licensed builds.
